title: Acid-Base Titration
question: How can an indicator reveal when an acid has been neutralized?
objective: Neutralize the acid without overshooting the endpoint
summary: Use an indicator and measured additions of base to find the neutral endpoint of an acid sample.

steps.title: Inspect the sample
steps.prompt: Begin by finding a safe way to make the acid-base change visible.

actions.label: Add indicator
actions.description: Add two drops of indicator to the acid sample.

actions.label: Heat the acid
actions.description: Warm the sample before measuring its pH.

steps.title: Prepare the burette
steps.prompt: Set up a controlled way to add base while observing the flask.

actions.label: Fill to the mark
actions.description: Fill the burette with base and note the starting volume.

actions.label: Pour from the bottle
actions.description: Pour the base directly into the flask in one motion.

steps.title: Find the endpoint
steps.prompt: Add base while watching for a color that remains after swirling.

actions.label: Add one drop at a time
actions.description: Slow the flow and swirl between drops near the endpoint.

actions.label: Open the stopcock fully
actions.description: Let a large amount of base rush into the flask.

actions.label: Stop before color holds
actions.description: Stop while the indicator color still vanishes after swirling.

steps.title: Record the result
steps.prompt: Choose the observation that best supports a neutral endpoint.

actions.label: Record the lasting pale color
actions.description: Record the final volume and the faint color that persists after swirling.

actions.label: Record the darkest color
actions.description: Choose the darkest color as the endpoint regardless of volume.

outcomes.title: A color appears
outcomes.message: The clear sample flashes pink, then settles back as it mixes.
outcomes.explanation: Indicator molecules change structure with pH, which changes the wavelengths of light they absorb.

outcomes.title: Endpoint overshot
outcomes.message: The flask turns a strong, lasting color: too much base was added.
outcomes.explanation: Past the equivalence region, excess hydroxide controls the pH. The trial can be repeated with smaller additions; failures are data.

outcomes.title: Endpoint recorded
outcomes.message: Your measured addition produced a faint color that persists after swirling.
outcomes.explanation: A persistent indicator change near the expected endpoint provides evidence that acid and base reacted in equivalent amounts.
